body.title: CLOSED: audit-ticket two-sided-build check — enforce capability tickets ship handler + skill consumer
body.result: Added Check 16 (two-sided-build) to audit-ticket SKILL.md. Triggers on capability-tagged tickets or MCP/shim/handler keywords; verifies both handler + skill-consumer files in Affected files; SPLITs with sequencing if only one side; honors explicit exemption line. Test cases documented in skill for CC.1 integration. Synced to agent_datacenter master + pushed (3bc3b30).
body.description:
**Approved 2026-05-03 (Akien — 'all is approved! go!').** Enforcement ticket for theigors/rules/capability-protocol/two-sided-build. Without filing-time enforcement, capability tickets will drift back to single-sided shipping and the rule rots.

**Filing-time check:** when a ticket's tags include 'Capability' OR its description mentions 'MCP capability' / 'shim' / 'handler', audit-ticket verifies the Affected files list includes BOTH at least one shim/handler file (e.g., agent_datacenter/devices/, lab/claudecode/mcp_*) AND at least one skill file (/home/akien/.claude/skills/*). If only one side is present, audit-ticket returns SPLIT with a recommended sequencing (handler first, then consumer with explicit gate).

No handler change — this is pure skill-consumer-side work modifying /audit-ticket. Therefore exempt from theigors/rules/capability-protocol/two-sided-build itself (it's the enforcer, not a consumer of a new capability).

**Affected files:** /home/akien/.claude/skills/audit-ticket/SKILL.md (add the two-sided-build check as a new step or extend the design-rules check)
**Design rules:** theigors/rules/capability-protocol/two-sided-build (the rule being enforced), theigors/rules/ticket_design_checks (audit-ticket's home for filing-time positive checks).
**Scope boundary:** IN: pattern-match for capability tickets, two-sided file-check, SPLIT recommendation with handler-first sequencing. OUT: detection beyond Affected files inspection (no AST analysis of the actual code). OUT: silently auto-splitting tickets (always returns SPLIT for human review, never modifies).
**Test plan:** unit test for the check (capability ticket with both sides → PASS; capability ticket with only handler → SPLIT; capability ticket with only skill → SPLIT; non-capability ticket → check skipped). CC.1 integration test that runs audit-ticket against three fixture tickets and asserts the right return value for each.
